content:
I have been getting familiar with the Linux Kernel and wanted to get into driver development. So far I have been doing this inside a Vmware instance on my HP laptop. I have decided to look into Nvidia device drivers. But the problem is my laptop isn't that good at power management, after the Bios update it shows a garbage value from one of the temperature sensors, I know because I was investigating dmesg output. I couldn't downgrade the Bios.
I noticed a huge difference in heat dissipation from the laptop body when running on Windows vs Linux currently on v6.17, tried v7.0rc2 still not much of a difference.
I noticed on Linux the power consumption was too erratic jumps to 40w randomly. Temps stay in sub 60°C on idle.
The wifi card, ssd and the RAM, all show unusually high temps and I can feel it too.
Did some research I found HP has a proprietary driver that handles power management, temps on idle around 40°C.
Am I screwed with this paper weight now or can I create a patch series fixing this issue for everyone?
Laptop model 16-n0123AX

can I create a patch series fixing this issue for everyone?

If you have a patch that fixes it, then definitely please send it upstream -- anyone can send patches, and that would be a nice contribution. I would suggest contacting the upstream maintainers. about it.
shivamklr has marked this topic as resolved.
